name: @chbrain/khai-engine-totem
description: khai engine content, the totem domain: a sacred object set apart from the profane that stands for the group -- what it concentrates, who is bound to it, and what its charging and violation do.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Totem as piece: a sacred object set apart from the profane that stands for the group -- the emblem that concentrates a collective's force, the adherent bound to revere it, and the charging and violation that make and break the sacred.

card.wire: Attaches at Piece. The anchor (piece_totem.md) declares the totem as what it is in the room: an object set apart as sacred that stands for the group, holding a force out of all proportion to its substance. The law makes the sacred a thing the scene can carry and profane.
card.issue: The totem, read beneath the object. One position names who is bound to it (the adherent who reveres it); two processes name what makes and breaks its sacredness -- consecration (the gathered group charges it) and desecration (it is profaned, and sanction follows). The object is the piece; the members are the force it concentrates.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so an object marked sacred is read as set apart wherever it sits. The link: the persona bound to it links the adherent stance and the process it is in under Projection.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real adherent position or totem process is linked. Whether the object truly holds the sacred force the scene needs is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then set the totem: name the object, the group it stands for, who reveres it, and which process it is in -- being charged by the gathering, or profaned by violation. Read it in the disproportion between what the object is and what it commands.
